sourceMovie.title: Ladies First
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Few comedy from 2026 stuck like Ladies First—it found the nerve that matters and pressed on it without letting up. Thea Sharrock understood the assignment. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of in that era.

recommendations.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Think of Crazy, Stupid, Love. (2011) as a lateral move from Ladies First—the comedy priorities are the same, the story is entirely different. The comic timing and sharper banter is what seals the pairing.

recommendations.title: The Hangover Part III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Viewers who connected with Ladies First's comedy core tend to find The Hangover Part III (2013) almost immediately. The comic timing and sharper banter is recognisable even when the characters and plot are entirely new.

recommendations.title: Idiocracy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Idiocracy (2006) will feel intuitive to anyone who responded to Ladies First's comedy approach. The comic timing and sharper banter plays differently here—same frequency, new transmission.

recommendations.title: Nothing to Hide
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nothing to Hide (2018) understands that comedy works best when it trusts the audience—the same principle that drives Ladies First. Both films close on comic timing and sharper banter you won't shake quickly.

recommendations.title: Bananas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ladies First uses comedy to get under your skin; Bananas (1971) works the same seam with different tools. The comic timing and sharper banter is the reward both films share.

recommendations.title: Wild Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Both Ladies First and Wild Target (2010) insist on comedy without softening the edges. The comic timing and sharper banter is the payoff fans of either film tend to cite first.

recommendations.title: Like a Boss
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Like a Boss (2020) channels the comedy energy of Ladies First into a completely different story. Expect the same comic timing and sharper banter, earned through its own logic rather than borrowed from the first watch.

recommendations.title: Father There Is Only One 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Father There Is Only One 2 (2020) understands that comedy works best when it trusts the audience—the same principle that drives Ladies First. Both films close on comic timing and sharper banter you won't shake quickly.

recommendations.title: About Fate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: About Fate (2022) occupies the same tonal register as Ladies First: committed comedy, comic timing and sharper banter, and zero interest in making things easier than they need to be.

recommendations.title: Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Both Ladies First and Good Luck, Have Fun, Don't Die (2026) insist on comedy without softening the edges. The comic timing and sharper banter is the payoff fans of either film tend to cite first.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ladies First?
faq.answer: Crazy, Stupid, Love. first—it's the tightest match—then Stupid and Love. and The Hangover Part III and Idiocracy branch out from there.

faq.question: What genre is Ladies First?
faq.answer: Strictly it's Comedy, but Ladies First plays like it knows what genre it belongs to and pushes against it. That tension is part of the appeal.

faq.question: Is Ladies First worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
